items.title: USDA and Ohio issue violation notices to 19 SNAP retailers in fraud crackdown
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Administration and Ohio’s Investigative Unit issued formal SNAP violation notices to 19 retailers across Cleveland, Cincinnati, and Columbus on June 3–4, 2026. USDA said one store allegedly trafficked more than $14,000 in SNAP benefits, and described another case where a retailer exchanged more than $800 in benefits for $300 in cash and other ineligible items.

items.title: HUD announces $4.04B FY2026 Continuum of Care homelessness funding and new priorities
items.summary: HUD announced a new Notice of Funding Opportunity for the Continuum of Care (CoC) program totaling $4.04 billion for FY 2026. HUD said $1.3 billion of the total is for new projects, and emphasized priorities including transitional housing and supportive services alongside housing assistance.

items.title: USDA updates WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash benefit amounts for FY2026
items.summary: USDA set FY 2026 monthly WIC cash-value benefits at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants. USDA said the FY 2026 inflation adjustment is 11.8% over the FY 2022 baseline, and noted states were required to authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026.

items.title: Medicaid work requirements: KFF tracker highlights early state start dates ahead of 2027 federal requirement
items.summary: KFF reports that a 2025 reconciliation law requires states to condition Medicaid eligibility for ACA expansion adults on work requirements starting January 1, 2027. KFF notes Nebraska began enforcing requirements early on May 1, 2026, and that Montana (July 1, 2026) and Iowa (December 1, 2026) plan early implementation; Arkansas plans a July 1, 2026 “soft” launch without disenrollments before 2027.

items.title: HUD sets Jan. 1, 2027 deadline for most PHAs to use HOTMA-compliant HUD-50058 reporting
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-15 says PHAs (with exceptions including MTW PHAs and PHAs exclusively using FRS) must reflect HOTMA requirements in all HUD-50058 transactions with an effective date on or after January 1, 2027. HUD also notes that PHAs that start annual reexams 120 days in advance may need to begin collecting/verifying income under HOTMA policies starting in September 2026.

items.title: IRS details expanded employer-provided child care tax credit rules for tax year 2026+
items.summary: IRS guidance says the employer-provided child care credit for amounts paid or incurred after December 31, 2025 equals 40% of qualified child care expenditures (50% for eligible small businesses) plus 10% of qualified child care resource and referral expenditures. IRS says the annual credit cap increases to $500,000 (or $600,000 for eligible small businesses), with both amounts adjusted for inflation.

items.title: IRS Low Income Taxpayer Clinic (LITC) matching grant applications open through July 6, 2026
items.summary: IRS says it is accepting applications for Low Income Taxpayer Clinic matching grants from May 6, 2026 through July 6, 2026. The program supports organizations that help lower-income taxpayers resolve disputes with the IRS and understand their rights and responsibilities.

items.title: SSA data: average disability claim processing time was 193 days in Feb. 2026
items.summary: SSA’s open data shows the average processing time for combined initial Title II disability and Title XVI blind/disabled claims was 193 days in February 2026 (FY 2026). The same dataset shows a FY 2026-to-date average of 196 days through February.
